questions.text: Acetyl CoA completely oxidizes to Carbon dioxide and liberates _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: When acetyl CoA undergoes complete oxidation in the citric acid cycle (also known as the Krebs cycle or tricarboxylic acid cycle), it liberates carbon dioxide and transfers high-energy electrons to carrier molecules. Acetyl CoA is oxidized to carbon dioxide (CO2). Electrons are transferred to carrier molecules NAD+ and FAD, forming NADH and FADH2. A small amount of energy is captured in the form of GTP, which can later be converted to ATP.

When acetyl CoA undergoes complete oxidation in the citric acid cycle (also known as the Krebs cycle or tricarboxylic acid cycle), it liberates carbon dioxide and transfers high-energy electrons to carrier molecules. A small amount of energy is captured in the form of GTP, which can later be converted to ATP.

A small amount of energy is captured in the form of GTP, which can later be converted to ATP.

When Acetyl-CoA is completely oxidized in the citric acid cycle (also called the Krebs cycle or tricarboxylic acid cycle), it breaks down into carbon dioxide (CO₂) and releases energy. During this process, high-energy electrons are transferred to molecules called NAD⁺ and FAD, forming NADH and FADH₂. These energy-rich molecules then carry electrons to the electron transport chain, where more ATP is produced. In total, each turn of the cycle produces 3 NADH, 1 FADH₂, and 1 ATP (or GTP), along with 2 molecules of CO₂.

questions.explanation: When acetyl CoA undergoes complete oxidation in the citric acid cycle (also known as the Krebs cycle or tricarboxylic acid cycle), it liberates carbon dioxide and transfers high-energy electrons to carrier molecules. The carbon dioxide produced is a byproduct of the decarboxylation reactions that occur during the citric acid cycle.

questions.text: In which of the following components of the body does lactic acid fermentation take place?

options.explanation: The brain primarily relies on aerobic respiration and does not typically undergo lactic acid fermentation due to its critical demand for oxygen.

The liver is involved in processing and converting lactic acid back into glucose (Cori cycle) rather than accumulating it.
During intense exercise, when oxygen levels are insufficient, lactic acid fermentation occurs in skeletal muscles, leading to the accumulation of lactic acid and resulting in muscle fatigue.

questions.explanation: Lactic acid fermentation primarily occurs in skeletal muscles under anaerobic conditions, such as during intense exercise when oxygen supply is limited. Pyruvate, a product of glycolysis, is converted to lactic acid, leading to its accumulation and subsequent muscle fatigue. The brain, heart, and liver do not typically undergo lactic acid fermentation. The brain relies heavily on aerobic respiration for energy due to its high oxygen requirement. The heart muscles are designed to resist fatigue and maintain continuous aerobic respiration. The liver's role is to convert lactic acid back to glucose, not to accumulate it.

questions.text: Fermentation is

options.text: Fermentation occurs in the presence of oxygen
options.explanation: This statement is false because fermentation does not require oxygen. It occurs in the absence of oxygen, which is why it is known as an anaerobic process. Organisms such as yeast and certain bacteria use fermentation when oxygen is unavailable to continue producing energy. If oxygen were present, these organisms would switch to aerobic respiration instead, which yields more energy.

options.text: Fermentation occurs in the absence of oxygen
options.explanation: This statement is true because fermentation is an anaerobic process. It allows cells to generate energy without using oxygen by partially breaking down glucose molecules. During this process, small amounts of ATP are produced, and byproducts such as lactic acid or ethanol are formed depending on the type of organism. This helps organisms survive in oxygen-poor environments.

options.text: Fermentation produces large amounts of energy
options.explanation: This statement is false because fermentation produces very little energy compared to aerobic respiration. It yields only two molecules of ATP per molecule of glucose, which is far less than the energy produced when oxygen is used. Because of this, organisms relying on fermentation must break down more glucose to meet their energy needs.

options.text: Fermentation completely breaks down glucose into carbon dioxide and water
options.explanation: This statement is false because fermentation only partially breaks down glucose. Complete breakdown of glucose into carbon dioxide and water happens during aerobic respiration, not fermentation. In fermentation, the process stops earlier, producing compounds like lactic acid or ethanol, which still contain much of the energy from the original glucose.

questions.explanation: Fermentation is a type of anaerobic respiration (without oxygen) in which glucose or other carbohydrates are broken down incompletely. It releases energy in the form of ATP and produces byproducts like lactic acid (in animals) or ethanol and CO₂ (in yeast). Unlike aerobic respiration, it does not fully oxidize glucose into carbon dioxide and water.

questions.text: When path difference between two waves are odd integral multiple of half the wavelength, the resultant effect is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Destructive interference occurs when two waves with opposite phases (180 degrees out of phase) combine. In this case, the crests of one wave align with the troughs of the other wave, resulting in a cancellation of amplitudes. As a result, the resultant wave has a smaller amplitude or even zero amplitude at certain points.This phenomenon is commonly observed in various wave systems, such as sound waves, light waves, and water waves. It is characterized by the reduction or complete elimination of wave amplitudes at specific locations where destructive interference occurs.

Constructive interference occurs when two waves meet in such a way that their displacements reinforce each other, resulting in an increase in the overall amplitude of the resulting wave. This reinforcement happens when the path difference between the waves is an even multiple of half the wavelength. In this case, the crests of both waves coincide, as do the troughs, leading to a wave with greater amplitude.

Beats occur when two waves of slightly different frequencies interfere with each other. The resultant wave alternates between constructive and destructive interference as the phases of the two waves periodically align and misaligned. This periodic variation in the intensity or amplitude of the resulting wave produces a sound that is perceived as a "beat" or pulsation.

Diffraction is the bending of waves around obstacles or the spreading of waves as they pass through openings. It occurs when waves encounter an obstruction or aperture that is comparable in size to their wavelength. Diffraction can result in the spreading out of waves, leading to phenomena such as interference patterns or the bending of light around corners.

questions.text: Which one of the following factors has no effect on the speed of sound in a gas?

options.explanation: Humidity refers to the amount of moisture or water vapor present in the air. It does have an effect on the speed of sound. Generally, as humidity increases, the speed of sound in a gas decreases. This is because water vapor molecules in humid air can slow down the transmission of sound waves.

Pressure is the force exerted by a gas per unit area. Interestingly, pressure does not have a direct effect on the speed of sound in a gas. Changes in pressure primarily affect the density of the gas, which indirectly influences the speed of sound.

Temperature plays a significant role in determining the speed of sound in a gas. As the temperature increases, the speed of sound also increases. This is because higher temperatures cause gas molecules to move faster, leading to faster sound wave propagation.
